steps.title: Spot the causal link
steps.content: The key signal is that extinction factors are “directly tied to the presence of humans”—the passage explicitly establishes that humans caused the biodiversity decrease.

steps.title: Match causation to the choices
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean "caused" — humans' arrival is thought to have set off the biodiversity decrease, and extinction factors are directly tied to human presence. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — followed: "humans followed a sudden decrease" — followed means came after; this reverses causation, making the decrease precede humans. ✗
  • Choice B — prolonged: "humans prolonged a sudden decrease" — prolonged means extended the duration; but the passage says humans caused it, not extended it. ✗
  • Choice C — counteracted: "humans counteracted a sudden decrease" — counteracted means worked against or offset; the passage says humans caused the decrease, not prevented it. ✗
  • Choice D — triggered: "humans triggered a sudden decrease" — triggered means initiated or set off a chain of events, exactly expressing human causation of biodiversity loss. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
